title: Iron And Steel Usage
point: Iron and steel have been used by mankind for nearly 6000 years.

title: Modern Steel Industry
point: Modern iron and steel industry emerged during the 19th century.

title: World Steel Production 1950
point: World steel production was 189 MT in 1950.

title: World Steel Production 1970
point: World steel production reached 595 MT by 1970.

title: Growth Driver Post WWII
point: Steel industry expanded rapidly after the Second World War.

title: Slow Growth Period
point: Steel production growth slowed during 1970–2000.

title: Static Demand Impact
point: Static demand in developed economies reduced steel production growth.

title: Oil Crisis Impact
point: 1970s oil crisis negatively affected global steel growth.

title: Afghanistan War Impact
point: 1980s Afghanistan war contributed to slower steel growth.

title: Asian Crisis Impact
point: 1997 Asian currency crisis impacted steel demand.

title: China Overtakes USA
point: China overtook the USA in steel production in 1993.

title: China Overtakes Japan
point: China overtook Japan in steel production in 1996.

title: China Production 2000
point: China produced 131 MT crude steel in 2000.

title: China Production 2022
point: China produced 1013 MT crude steel in 2022.

title: China Share 2022
point: China accounted for 54% of world steel production in 2022.

title: Global Crisis 2008
point: Steel production dipped during the 2008 global financial crisis.

title: China Clampdown 2016
point: Steel production dipped in 2016 due to China's production restrictions.

title: Global Capacity 2025
point: Global steelmaking capacity reached 2.55 billion metric tons by 2025.

title: OECD Warning
point: Global steel overcapacity may exceed 680 million metric tons by 2025.

title: Steel Demand Recovery
point: Global steel demand may recover by 1% in 2026.

title: China Demand Trend
point: Steel demand in China continues to decline.

title: Growth Economies
point: India and ASEAN nations are expected to sustain steel demand growth.

title: Ancient Civilizations
point: Iron was used in Babylon

title: Archaeological Evidence
point: Mesopotamia and Egypt provide evidence of ancient iron usage.

title: Bronze Preference
point: Bronze was preferred until iron processing techniques improved.

title: Early Fuel
point: Charcoal from wood was initially used for iron smelting.

title: Coal Discovery
point: Coal later became a major heat source for iron production.

title: Coke Development
point: Coal was converted into coke for efficient smelting.

title: Saugus Works
point: Saugus Works was the first successful iron works in America.

title: Saugus Year
point: Saugus Works was founded in 1646.

title: Bessemer Process
point: The Bessemer Process was invented in 1856.

title: Bessemer Impact
point: Bessemer Process enabled low-cost mass steel production.

title: Iron Pillar
point: The Iron Pillar near New Delhi demonstrates ancient metallurgical excellence.

title: Konark Temple
point: Massive iron beams were used in the Sun Temple at Konark.

title: Rig Veda Reference
point: Rig Veda contains references to iron usage.

title: Atharva Veda Reference
point: Atharva Veda contains references to iron usage.

title: Indian Iron Production
point: India has produced iron for more than 3000 years.

title: World Production 2025
point: World crude steel production was 1849.4 MT in 2025.

title: India Ranking 2025
point: India was the second-largest steel producer in 2025.

title: China Production 2025
point: China produced 960.8 MT crude steel in 2025.

title: India Production 2025
point: India produced 164.9 MT crude steel in 2025.

title: India Growth 2025
point: India's steel production grew by 10.4% in 2025.

title: US Production 2025
point: USA produced 82 MT crude steel in 2025.

title: Japan Production 2025
point: Japan produced 80.7 MT crude steel in 2025.

title: Russia Production 2025
point: Russia produced 67.8 MT crude steel in 2025.

title: South Korea Production 2025
point: South Korea produced 61.9 MT crude steel in 2025.

title: Germany Production 2025
point: Germany produced 34.1 MT crude steel in 2025.

title: Brazil Production 2025
point: Brazil produced 33.3 MT crude steel in 2025.

title: Iran Production 2025
point: Iran produced 31.8 MT crude steel in 2025.

title: Top Producer
point: China remained the world's largest steel producer in 2025.

title: World Demand 2025
point: Global steel demand in 2025 is projected at 1749 MT.

title: World Demand 2026
point: Global steel demand in 2026 is projected at 1773 MT.

title: Demand Rebound
point: A modest global steel demand rebound is expected in 2026.

title: Developing Economies
point: India Vietnam Egypt and Saudi Arabia support global demand growth.

title: Europe Demand
point: Steel demand growth is expected to return in Europe.

title: Ex-China Growth
point: Demand excluding China may grow 3.4% in 2025.

title: Ex-China Growth 2026
point: Demand excluding China may grow 4.7% in 2026.

title: Africa Demand Growth
point: Africa steel demand grew about 5.5% annually.

title: Africa Demand 2025
point: Africa steel demand reached around 41 MT in 2025.

title: India Capacity Growth
point: India is expanding steelmaking capacity rapidly.

title: India Global Rank
point: India ranks second globally in crude steel production.

title: Capacity 2025
point: India's crude steel capacity reached 200 MT in 2025.

title: Capacity Target 2030
point: India targets 300 MT steel capacity by 2030.

title: FY18 Production
point: India produced about 103 MT crude steel in FY18.

title: FY26 Production
point: India's crude steel production may reach 167 MT by FY26.

title: FY18 Consumption
point: India consumed about 92 MT steel in FY18.

title: FY26 Consumption
point: India's steel consumption may reach 161 MT by FY26.

title: Recovery Driver
point: Infrastructure construction and manufacturing drove post-FY21 recovery.

title: Domestic Absorption
point: Gap between production and consumption has narrowed.

title: High Growth Market
point: India is emerging as a major high-growth steel market.

title: FY25 Production Growth
point: Crude steel production increased 5.3% in FY25.

title: FY25 Consumption Growth
point: Finished steel consumption increased 11.5% in FY25.

title: FY25 Import Growth
point: Finished steel imports increased 14.8% in FY25.

title: FY25 Export Decline
point: Finished steel exports declined 35.1% in FY25.

title: Demand Driven Expansion
point: Indian steel growth is increasingly demand-driven.

title: Finished Steel FY25
point: Finished steel production reached 146.6 MT in FY25.

title: Consumption FY25
point: Finished steel consumption reached 152 MT in FY25.

title: Import FY25
point: Finished steel imports were 9.6 MT in FY25.

title: Export FY25
point: Finished steel exports were 4.9 MT in FY25.

title: Market Leaders
point: JSW Steel and Tata Steel are market leaders.

title: JSW Share
point: JSW Steel held 13.9% market share.

title: Tata Share
point: Tata Steel held 13.6% market share.

title: SAIL Share
point: SAIL held 10.6% market share.

title: Other Producers Share
point: Other producers accounted for 44.5% market share.

title: Import Share
point: Imports represented 6.3% market share.

title: Market Fragmentation
point: Indian steel market is highly diversified.

title: EAF Route
point: EAF stands for Electric Arc Furnace route.

title: IF Route
point: IF stands for Induction Furnace route.

title: Oxygen Route Share FY25
point: Oxygen route accounted for 41% of production.

title: EAF Share FY25
point: EAF route accounted for 21% of production.

title: IF Share FY25
point: IF route accounted for 38% of production.

title: IF Route Growth
point: IF route increased from 35% to 38%.

title: Oxygen Route Decline
point: Oxygen route declined from 43% to 41%.

title: EAF Route Decline
point: EAF route declined from 22% to 21%.

title: BF-BOF Preference
point: Major steel companies prefer BF-BOF for expansion.

title: Demand CAGR
point: India's steel consumption may grow at 5–6% CAGR.

title: Demand 2035
point: Steel demand may reach 240–260 MT by 2035.

title: National Infrastructure Pipeline
point: India's infrastructure pipeline is valued at $1.4 trillion.

title: Capacity Addition Plan
point: Steel companies announced about 70 MT capacity additions.

title: Future Demand Drivers
point: Automotive renewables and defense will drive future steel demand.
